     ARTICLE 7. All laws or parts of laws’ in conflict with

                                    S. S. ROBISON,
                           Rear-Admiral, United States Navy,

Sdnto Domingo, 13. E.,
June 20,1921.

     MILITARY GOVERNMENT O F SANTO DOMINGO..
                    Executive Order No. 639.
              Amending Executive Order No. 637.
                       0. G. No. 3233.

    By virtue of the powers vested in the Military Government
of Santo Domingo, the following Executive Order is hereby pro-
mulgated :
    ARTICLE 1. Article 12 of Executive Order No. . 637, is
hereby amended to read a i follows :
    “ARTICLE 12. WITH T H E CONSENT O F THE UNIT-
E D STATES, THERE IS SECURED THE ACCEPTANGE O F
AND VALIDATION O F THIS BOND ISSUE BY THE GOV-
ERNMENT O F THE DOMINICAN REPUBLIC, AS A LEGAL,
BINDING AND IRREVOCABLE OBLIGATION O F T H E DO-
MINICAN REPUBLIC, AND T H E DUTIES O F THE GENE-
RAL RECEIVER O F DOMINICAN CUSTOMS, AS PROV-
IDED UNDER THE AMERICAN-DOMINICAN CQNVEN-
TI3 ‘ O F 1907, ARE EXTENDED TO THIS BOND ISSUE.”
       ARTICLE 2. In order to make the English version of
Executive Order No. 637 correspond with the Spanish version,
t h e former is corrected by adding t o the end of Article 15 the
followhg sentence :
     “A like agreement shall be necessary to modify the impoh-
duties.”
    ARTICLE 3. All laws o r parts of laws in conflict herewith,

                                431-

 a r e hereby repealed.

                                       S. S. ROBISON,
                              R,ear-Admiral, United States Navy,
                             Military Governor of Santo Domingo.
 Santo Doming0 City, D. R.,
 June 25, 1921.

     MILITARY GOVERNMENT O F SANTO DOMINGO.
                     Executive Order No. 640.

          Appropriation towards the Completion of the
              Santo Domingo-Monte Cristy Road.
                        0. G. No. 3233.

    By virtue of the powers vested in the Military Government
of Santo Domingo, the following Executive Order is hereby pro-
mulgated :
    There is hereby appropriated from funds of the Nacional
Treasury, not otherwise appropriated, the sum of FIVE HUND-
RED THOUSAND DOL'LARS ($500.000) for the construction
work on the Santo Domingo-Monte Cristy Road.

                                    S. S. ROBISON,
                           Rear-Admiral, United States Navy,
                          Military Governor of Santo Domingo.

Santo Domingo City, D. R.,
June 27, 1921.

    MILITARY GOVERNMENT O F SANTO DOMINGO.
